skill.name: ai-image-generation
skill.description: Generate AI images with GPT-Image-2, FLUX, Gemini, Grok, Seedream, Reve and 50+ models via inference.sh CLI
skill.summary: Generate AI images with 50+ models via inference.sh CLI
skill.author: inference-sh-skills

security_audit.summary: All static findings are false positives. The skill is documentation for the belt CLI tool. The external_commands detections are markdown code blocks showing legitimate CLI usage. Network detections are documentation URLs. The weak cryptographic algorithm flag is a misidentification. No malicious patterns detected.

medium_findings.title: Markdown Code Blocks Misidentified as Shell Execution
medium_findings.description: Static analyzer detected 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' but these are markdown code blocks (```bash ... ```) documenting belt CLI commands. The skill is documentation, not executable code. Commands use properly quoted JSON parameters with no shell injection risk.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Verified by reading SKILL.md - all commands are inside markdown code blocks with proper JSON quoting

low_findings.title: Documentation URLs Present
low_findings.description: Hardcoded URLs point to legitimate documentation for inference.sh service. These are normal documentation links.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: URLs verified to point to inference.sh official documentation

low_findings.title: Spurious Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Flag
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged line 3 as 'weak cryptographic algorithm' but line 3 is YAML frontmatter with no cryptographic content. This appears to be a false detection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: YAML frontmatter contains only metadata, no crypto algorithms present

content.value_statement: Creating AI-generated images requires accessing multiple APIs with different parameters. This skill provides a unified CLI interface to generate images with GPT-Image-2, FLUX, Gemini, and 50+ other models.
actual_capabilities: Generate images from text prompts with 50+ AI models
Edit existing images using natural language instructions
Perform inpainting to modify specific image regions
Upscale and enhance image quality using professional tools
Create images with accurate text rendering using Reve and Seedream
Generate product photos, concept art, and marketing visuals
limitations: Requires inference.sh account and belt CLI installation
Image quality and generation speed depend on selected model
Text rendering accuracy varies by model
Some models may have content policy restrictions

use_cases.title: Product Visualization
use_cases.description: Generate professional product photos and mockups for e-commerce listings and marketing campaigns.

use_cases.title: Concept Art Creation
use_cases.description: Create concept art, illustrations, and creative visuals for projects and presentations.

use_cases.title: Social Media Graphics
use_cases.description: Generate engaging visuals for social media posts, banners, and promotional content.
use_cases.target_user: Social media managers and content creators

prompt_templates.title: Basic Text-to-Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a [subject description] image in [style] style
prompt_templates.scenario: Simple image generation with a single model

prompt_templates.title: Product Photo
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a professional product photo of [product name] with [lighting style] lighting on a [background]
prompt_templates.scenario: E-commerce product imagery using FLUX or GPT-Image-2

prompt_templates.title: Image Editing
prompt_templates.prompt: Use GPT-Image-2 to edit [image_url] by [modification instruction]
prompt_templates.scenario: Modify existing images with inpainting or outpainting

prompt_templates.title: High-Resolution Output
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a [description] image using Seedream 4.5, then upscale with Topaz upscaler

output_examples.input: Generate a professional product photo of sneakers in studio lighting
output_examples.output: High-quality image of sneakers on a clean white background with professional studio lighting

output_examples.input: Create a cyberpunk city at night with neon lights
output_examples.output: Detailed cyberpunk-style illustration showing a futuristic cityscape with glowing neon signs

output_examples.input: Edit this image to change the background to a beach at sunset
output_examples.output: Original subject with new beach sunset background composited naturally

best_practices: Use specific, detailed prompts with style descriptors for better results
Select models based on your needs: FLUX for quality, FLUX Klein for speed, Seedream for text rendering
Chain commands with Topaz upscaler for production-quality output
anti_patterns: Avoid vague prompts like 'make it look good' - be specific about style, lighting, and composition
Do not assume all models support the same parameters - check model-specific requirements
Avoid generating harmful or policy-violating content that may be blocked

faq.question: What is the best model for text rendering?
faq.answer: Seedream 3.0 and Reve models are designed for accurate text rendering in images.

faq.question: How do I get the highest quality output?
faq.answer: Use Seedream 4.5 or ImagineArt 1.5 Pro for 4K quality, then apply Topaz upscaler for professional enhancement.

faq.question: What is the fastest model for quick iterations?
faq.answer: FLUX Klein 4B and P-Image models offer the fastest generation times with low cost.

faq.question: Can I edit existing images?
faq.answer: Yes, GPT-Image-2 and Reve support image editing, inpainting, and image-to-image transformations.

faq.question: Do I need an inference.sh account?
faq.answer: Yes, you need to install the belt CLI and log in with your inference.sh credentials.

faq.question: What is the cost of generating images?
faq.answer: Costs vary by model. FLUX Klein is very economical at $0.0001 per image. Check inference.sh for current pricing.
